memories.memory_text: I heard the wood groan, saw that skull move—just a dead thing twitching—and I put an arrow through its head before it could do anything else. One shot, clean through the skull.
grounded_facts: Cholo heard groaning wood and saw a skeleton move in the garbage pit.
He immediately used his crossbow to shoot the skeleton in the skull.
The arrow went completely through the white skull.
He said 'Poor bastard' before firing.

memories.memory_text: I jumped out of the van with the three guerrillas, looked at Riley, and asked, 'Ready?'—then fired up my bike and took off, yelling 'YEEEE-HAAAAAAAA!'
grounded_facts: Cholo arrived at Dead Reckoning in a white moving van with three guerrillas.
Cholo asked 'Ready?' to prompt departure.
Cholo mounted a motorcycle, revved the engine, and rode out ahead.
Cholo shouted 'YEEEE-HAAAAAAAA!' as he left.

memories.memory_text: I shot Riley a sarcastic line about not having any fun after he gave me that irritated look—like he’s got the right to judge how we run things.
grounded_facts: Cholo responds to Riley's irritated look with sarcasm, saying 'Hey, not me, Boss. You told me not to have any fun, I'm not having any fun at all.'

memories.memory_text: I told Mike to come with me and showed him the ropes by having him hop on the back of my bike—we’re heading out for supplies, and now he’s learning how things really work.
grounded_facts: Cholo says 'Come with me, kid. I'll show you the ropes.'
Mike climbs out of the T-Bird and gets on the back of Cholo's motorcycle as part of a supply run.

memories.memory_text: I didn’t wait for Riley to finish talking—I just walked into the liquor store with Foxy. The doors were wide open, and all I saw was darkness ahead, but that didn’t matter. Fifteen hundred a jug, and he wanted to walk away?
grounded_facts: Cholo entered the liquor store immediately after Riley objected.
Foxy accompanied Cholo into the store.
The store entrance was unobstructed and dark inside.
Cholo heard Foxy mention the high resale value of Kentucky whiskey.

memories.memory_text: I had both hands on the case of Dom Perignon when a third hand grabbed it—some dead thing with a swastika on its forehead lunging out of the dark. I punched it in the face while it tried to bite me, and for a second, I felt the weight of it crushing down on me.
grounded_facts: Cholo reached into the refrigerator and grabbed the case of Dom Perignon with both hands.
A zombie emerged from behind the refrigerator and grabbed the case and Cholo.
Cholo struggled with the zombie, punching it while it growled and tried to bite him.
Cholo was pinned under the zombie and the case of champagne during the struggle.

memories.memory_text: I saw a dead cop grab Mike's ankle and bite his leg. I shot it in the head with my crossbow. Then Mike put his gun under his chin and killed himself.
grounded_facts: A dead uniformed policeman grabbed Mike by the ankle and bit his leg.
Cholo shot the zombie in the head with his crossbow.
Mike committed suicide by shooting himself in the head with his .45.

memories.memory_text: I told Riley that we all know the risks when we take the job — Mike got bit, same as I would’ve, and his number came up. That’s just how it is.
grounded_facts: Cholo states that Mike's death was the result of knowing the risks of their work.
Cholo equates being bitten and killing oneself as an expected response.
Cholo asserts that every operative accepts the danger before going in.

memories.memory_text: I looked at that screen showing Fiddler's Green and told Riley I’m done following paths like my old man — I’ve got the money now, and I’m buying my way into my own place.
grounded_facts: Cholo references his father’s life of labor and lack of upward mobility.
Cholo declares his intent to purchase entry into Fiddler's Green.
Cholo connects his accumulated money to personal escape and status.
Cholo watches the monitor depicting Fiddler's Green while making this declaration.

memories.memory_text: I handed over my guns at the checkpoint, just like they wanted. Got a claim check back—same as everyone else going in clean.
grounded_facts: Cholo approaches the checkpoint with boxes of supplies.
He unstraps and hands over his weapons to the security guard.
The guard returns a claim check after taking the weapons.
Cholo collects his boxes and proceeds into Fiddler's Green.

memories.memory_text: I heard a scream down the corridor—sharp, panicked—so I dropped the champagne and Cohibas and started running.
grounded_facts: Cholo was delivering champagne and Cohibas to Penthouse Number One.
A scream was heard from down the corridor, followed by another.
Cholo dropped his delivery boxes upon hearing the scream.

memories.memory_text: The door to Penthouse Two was locked, and another scream came from inside—I threw myself at it, felt the latch splinter, and busted it open.
grounded_facts: Cholo reached the door of Penthouse Number Two and found it locked.
Another scream occurred while Cholo was at the door.
Cholo physically broke through the door by charging it, causing the latch to splinter.

memories.memory_text: I brought Kaufman champagne and cigars. I poured champagne into a highball glass and gave it to him, then lit two cigars—one for me and one for him. I told him I had enough money to buy a place in the Green. He said there was a long waiting list and that membership needed board approval.
grounded_facts: Cholo brought champagne and cigars to Kaufman.
Cholo poured champagne into a highball glass and gave it to Kaufman.
Cholo lit two cigars, one for himself and one for Kaufman.
Cholo stated he had enough money to buy a place in Fiddler's Green.
Kaufman responded that there was a long waiting list and that membership required board approval.

memories.memory_text: I grabbed Kaufman by the collar after he denied me, screamed that I'd cleaned up his garbage for three years and knew what was in it—he pressed a .45 into my side, still smiling, and called the guards to drag me out.
grounded_facts: Cholo lunged at Kaufman and grabbed his collar.
Cholo confronted Kaufman about years of service and disposing of his 'garbage'.
Kaufman drew a .45 pistol and pressed it against Cholo's waist.
Kaufman remained calm and smiling during the confrontation.
Security guards entered and physically removed Cholo from the room.

memories.memory_text: I spun fast, slugged one guard straight in the jaw, and felt his head snap back before I moved to the next.
grounded_facts: Cholo physically attacked a security guard by punching him in the face.
The attack was sudden and part of a rapid sequence of actions.

memories.memory_text: After dropping the third guard with a kick to the face, I bolted through the fire door—metal slamming behind me as I hit the street.
grounded_facts: Cholo kicked a guard in the face and then fled through a fire door.
The escape involved a clear exit from the stairwell into an exterior area.

memories.memory_text: I woke Foxy up in that run-down bedroom and told him we're taking the truck out — time to move. When he asked about Riley, I shut it down. Not him. Just the regular guys.
grounded_facts: Cholo wakes Foxy in a ratty bedroom.
Cholo announces they are taking the truck out and to get the guys.
Foxy asks about Riley.
Cholo explicitly excludes Riley, saying 'No, not him. Just the regular guys.'

memories.memory_text: I flashed the paper and told the guard I was taking the truck out—he hesitated when he saw it was dated yesterday, and I knew I had one second to decide whether to pull the gun or back down.
grounded_facts: Cholo presented a forged document to authorize removing the truck.
The depot guard noticed the document was outdated.
Cholo reached for his gun but did not draw it before being interrupted by gunfire.

memories.memory_text: I stopped my team from jumping into the firefight—Stenches were tearing into the guards, and I saw the opening. Told them, 'Ain't our fight,' then pointed us straight at the truck.
grounded_facts: Cholo hears screaming and gunfire, identifies the threat as Stenches attacking the depot.
Cholo's team begins preparing for combat, but Cholo stops them.
Cholo asserts control by declaring the battle not their concern and redirects the team to seize the truck.
Cholo leads the team sprinting toward Dead Reckoning.

memories.memory_text: I told Kaufman I'd blow him out of his ivory tower if he didn't hand over all the money in his bank—I've got Dead Reckoning, and that's what gives me the power.
grounded_facts: Cholo demanded his money and the rest of the money from Kaufman's bank.
Cholo issued a threat to 'blow' Kaufman out of his 'ivory tower' if the demand was not met.
Cholo asserted control by declaring 'I've got Dead Reckoning.'

memories.memory_text: I told Mouse to call me at midnight from the pier to let me know if we were rich or not—right after he jumped out and ran for the boat shed.
grounded_facts: Cholo instructed Mouse to call at midnight to report the financial outcome of the mission.
The instruction was given at the 10th Street Pier while Cholo stood in an open hatch of Dead Reckoning.
Mouse appeared nervous and ran to hide in a boat shed after receiving the order.

memories.memory_text: I watched the Gardener through the windshield, knowing if I didn't take that shot, I'd be just another disposable body to Kaufman. I fired the crossbow through the window—clean, one shot.
grounded_facts: Cholo observed the Gardener through the truck windshield.
Cholo referenced feeling no different than the 'poor Mexican bastard' if he didn't have control.
Cholo used a crossbow to shoot the Gardener in the head.
The action occurred at 11:20 PM, as shown on Cholo's watch.
Cholo believed the Gardener had no choice but to comply, implying stakes.

memories.memory_text: I checked my watch—11:29—and keyed the transmitter. 'Mouse. Anything?'
grounded_facts: Cholo looked at his watch and noted the time was 11:29.
Cholo used a transmitter to communicate with someone named Mouse.
Cholo initiated communication to check on mission status.

memories.memory_text: I opened the hatch and told Foxy to get out and check the place—gates were open, no one in sight, something felt off. Then I told someone to go help him, and Scar moved.
grounded_facts: Cholo activated the side hatch to open it.
Cholo directly ordered Foxy to exit the vehicle and investigate the site.
Cholo issued a follow-up command for someone to assist Foxy.
Scar responded to the command by preparing and offering to go.

memories.memory_text: I told them to stop when we had the Johnsons secured—enough was enough. The next move was already clear: we were going to blow up the Green.
grounded_facts: Cholo gave the command 'That's good. That's all we need.'
The Johnsons were physically restrained by Anchor and Scar in the Dead Reckoning.
Foxy stated the objective 'To blow up the Green.'
Cholo was present and in control during the capture of the Johnsons.

memories.memory_text: I saw Denbo and his guy in the road, told Pretty Boy to stop, took the mike and called out—Riley showed up outta nowhere with that Game Boy tracker, said they wanted in. I said 'Open up,' even though Foxy thought I was crazy.
grounded_facts: Cholo sees Denbo and an associate in the road ahead and orders the truck to stop.
Cholo uses a microphone to communicate with Riley, who has arrived in a T-Bird.
Riley reveals he tracked the truck using a transmitter and wants to join the operation.
Cholo instructs Pretty Boy to open the truck, overriding Foxy's objection.

memories.memory_text: I asked who else was out there, and when Riley admitted Kaufman’s men were dealt with, I confirmed the Johnsons were with us and ordered Pretty Boy to set the cannons.
grounded_facts: Cholo asked 'Who else is out there?'
Riley responded that Kaufman's men had been dealt with and mentioned Cholo picking up Johnsons at Ross Park
Cholo said 'Yup. Pretty Boy, set the cannons.'

memories.memory_text: Riley told me not to do it, but I told him straight—Kaufman's got it coming. I wasn't gonna back down, not after everything.
grounded_facts: Riley spoke directly to Cholo, urging him not to proceed.
Cholo responded with a definitive statement justifying his action against Kaufman.
The exchange occurred inside Dead Reckoning at night.
Cholo did not hesitate or show doubt in his response.

memories.memory_text: I remember telling them — he killed our friends, and every week we were the ones dumping bodies for Kaufman, doing his dirty work until he threw me away like trash.
grounded_facts: Cholo states that Kaufman killed many of their friends.
Cholo recalls regularly removing bodies ('taking out the garbage') for Kaufman.
Cholo expresses feeling used and discarded by Kaufman after being made into an enforcer.

memories.memory_text: I asked the kid if we're all just pretending to be alive—because sometimes it feels like surviving isn't the same as living.
grounded_facts: Riley speaks directly to someone referred to as 'son' in a reflective moment.
Riley questions the authenticity of their survival behavior, using the phrase 'Pretending to be alive?'

memories.memory_text: I turned and saw Charlie Houk step out of the shadows—half his face burned off, one eye bulging—and for a second, I braced for a fight, but then I recognized him. He was still sharp, still talking, and that’s when it hit me: if he could adapt, so could the walkers.
grounded_facts: Riley sees a heavily scarred figure emerge from the shadows and initially cannot identify it.
Riley recognizes the figure as Charlie Houk, a known but disfigured guerilla fighter.
Riley relaxes upon recognition, indicating prior familiarity.
Riley observes that Charlie, despite severe damage, remains intelligent and communicative.
Riley draws a comparison between Charlie’s adaptation and the walkers’ potential to learn and change.

memories.memory_text: I handed Harry a beer, and the next second he was on me—snarling, clawing—no warning, just pure animal. I smashed the bottle on Dead Reckoning and drove the glass into his skull before he choked the life out of me.
grounded_facts: Riley offered Harry a beer.
Harry suddenly attacked Riley without speaking.
Riley used a broken beer bottle to kill Harry in self-defense.
The attack happened near the vehicle Dead Reckoning.
Riley killed Harry before he could draw his gun.

memories.memory_text: I grabbed Cholo by the shirt and slammed him against the wall. Two people were dead, and Mike shouldn't have died for liquor and cigars.
grounded_facts: Riley physically confronts Cholo by grabbing his shirt and slamming him against the wall
Riley references Mike's death and expresses anger over the mission's purpose
The confrontation occurs in a subway tunnel with a TV monitor nearby
Cholo is holding Dom Perignon and Cohibas when Riley approaches

memories.memory_text: I looked at that monitor showing rich people in their club, and I knew—places like Fiddler's Green were never meant for us. We're the wrong kind.
grounded_facts: Riley observes a TV monitor displaying well-dressed people in a luxurious club setting
Riley states that people like him and Cholo would never be allowed into Fiddler's Green
The announcement mentions the city being protected by a private militia
Riley expresses a sense of exclusion based on class or identity

memories.memory_text: I told Mulligan 'Everyone's stoppable'—he was drunk on revolution, standing there with his whiskey, thinking numbers could bring down the Green. I knew better. No army fixes this place.
grounded_facts: Riley responded to Mulligan's revolutionary pitch with 'Everyone's stoppable.'
Mulligan was standing on a soapbox, offering whiskey and advocating for collective action against the Fiddler's Green high-rise.
Riley dismissed the idea of fixing the ghetto through force or uprising.

memories.memory_text: I saw the boy coughing behind Mulligan—pale, feverish—and I gave him the antibiotics. Didn't say much. Just pressed the bottle into Mulligan's hand and kept moving.
grounded_facts: Riley observed Mulligan's son Brian coughing and appearing ill.
Riley voluntarily gave a bottle of antibiotics to Mulligan for his son.
The act was silent and immediate, without fanfare or discussion.

memories.memory_text: I ripped off part of my shirt to bandage Slack's arm. I asked him why he was thrown into the arena. When he started telling me his story, I told him I didn't want to hear it—everybody's got a story, and I'm tired of hearing them.
grounded_facts: Riley rips part of his shirt-sleeve to bandage Slack's arm wound.
Riley asks Slack why he was thrown into the arena.
Slack explains he was punished for working with Mulligan.
Riley interrupts Slack, saying he's tired of hearing stories.

memories.memory_text: I told Slack I was going north to Canada—somewhere with no people. Then we saw Mulligan being chased and beaten by security men through the alley; I reached through the bars, but I couldn't do a damn thing.
grounded_facts: Riley states his intent to go to Canada, seeking a place without people.
Four security men chase Mulligan into the alley outside the jail cell.
Security men club and drag Mulligan away.
Riley instinctively reaches through the window bars but is unable to intervene.

memories.memory_text: The cell door clanged open and the guard demanded, 'Which one of you is Denbo?'—I froze for half a second, eyes locking with Charlie and Slack, knowing that name wasn't mine, but now it was a noose around my neck.
grounded_facts: A security guard opened a cell door and asked which prisoner was Denbo.
Riley exchanged glances with Charlie and Slack in response to the guard's question.
The moment created immediate tension, as Riley was not Denbo but was implicated by association or deception.

memories.memory_text: I led the team into the depot, M-16 up, scanning the smoke—bodies torn apart, fences pushed in from outside. These stenches are learning, working together like in Uniontown.
grounded_facts: Riley leads the team into the depot with his M-16 at the ready.
He observes signs of walker activity, including downed fences and body parts.
He states that the stenches are becoming more organized and intelligent, referencing prior experience in Uniontown.

memories.memory_text: After Charlie's shot, the growling in the smoke had everyone spooked—I grabbed the Motorola and keyed the radio. 'Riley Denbo. Calling the Green.'
grounded_facts: Riley requests a radio and receives a Motorola from Teahouse.
Following a walker attack and gunfire, Riley uses the radio to contact Fiddler's Green.
He transmits his name and calls out to the Green amid rising tension.

memories.memory_text: I pointed my gun at Pillsbury and Manolete, told them I was taking Dead Reckoning and going to Canada—no discussion, no hesitation. If they didn’t like it, the walkers in the marsh were their other option.
grounded_facts: Riley draws his weapon on Pillsbury and Manolete
Riley declares his intent to take Dead Reckoning and go to Canada
Riley forces compliance by implying the alternative is being left in a dangerous area with walkers

memories.memory_text: I held up the Game Boy unit, the red dot blinking on the screen—my homing device still working, tracking Dead Reckoning. Slack asked how I knew where to go, and I showed her: I built the damn thing to find it.
grounded_facts: Riley possesses a Game Boy-sized electronic unit with a blinking red dot on a map
The device is a homing beacon Riley built and implanted in Dead Reckoning
Riley reveals the device in response to Slack’s question about navigation
The device emits a soft beep as the dot updates on a new map

memories.memory_text: I told the team to stay put and watch the hatches—Dead Reckoning’s got steel skin too thick to shoot through, so I’m going in alone, hoping words get me inside.
grounded_facts: Riley instructed the team to remain in the shadows of the driveway.
Riley stated that Dead Reckoning has steel skin an inch thick.
Riley expressed intent to talk his way into Dead Reckoning.
Riley planned for a possible escape route by leaving a hatch open if he succeeded in entering.

memories.memory_text: I pressed another button on the Game Boy just as Cholo turned on me, his voice roaring over the silence that followed his failed attempt to start the system.
grounded_facts: Riley pushed a button on the 'Game Boy' device.
Cholo noticed the device in Riley's hand after failing to activate the system.
Cholo accused Riley of interfering using the 'Game Boy'.
The event occurred inside Dead Reckoning at night.

memories.memory_text: I told Cholo 'Not a chance,' and threw the device out the hatch—he lunged for it, but it was already gone.
grounded_facts: Riley refused Cholo's demand to fix the device.
Riley physically tossed the 'Game Boy' control device out of the open hatch.
Cholo reacted by reaching out toward the device after it was thrown.

memories.memory_text: I saw the gun barrel flash out of the corner of my eye—no time to shout, just push Cholo down and take the hit myself. The bullet tore into my shoulder as I hit the pavement.
grounded_facts: Riley saw the machine gun barrel flash in the moonlight.
Riley pushed Cholo out of the way of gunfire.
Riley was wounded in the shoulder during the act.
Riley dropped from the hatch onto the pavement after being shot.

memories.memory_text: I held Anchor and Scar at gunpoint while checking the clock—12:01—and keyed the transmitter to call the Green, my voice steady despite the chaos behind me.
grounded_facts: Riley unslung his M-16 and pointed it at Anchor and Scar to prevent them from drawing their guns.
Riley looked at the clock on the control panel, which read 12:01.
Riley activated the transmitter and announced, 'Riley Denbo calling the Green.'

memories.memory_text: I told him I was taking his fucking truck — no hesitation, just the way it had to be.
grounded_facts: Riley speaks these words in a decisive tone, asserting control over the situation.
The statement is directed at someone whose truck is being claimed, indicating a break in loyalty or hierarchy.
The phrase 'I'm taking your fucking truck' is explicitly stated in the script.

memories.memory_text: I saw the orange glow across the river and moved between the houses on the hillside to get a clear view—the city was on fire. The stenches had crossed the river. They actually got across the fucking river.
grounded_facts: Riley observed an orange glow coming from across the river.
Riley positioned himself between hillside houses to see the city burning.
Riley realized the stenches had crossed the river, which surprised and stunned him.

memories.memory_text: I told them to take the T-Bird—guns and ammo inside—and said they'd be safer coming with us. Cholo refused, but I made sure they had a chance.
grounded_facts: Riley instructed Cholo and Foxy about the location of the T-Bird.
Riley informed them there were guns and ammo inside the T-Bird.
Riley stated Cholo and Foxy would be safer joining their group.

memories.memory_text: Slack tore off her bandana and used it to wipe the blood from my neck—her hands steady, her eyes sharp. I didn’t flinch, but I felt it, that quiet moment where she almost lost me.
grounded_facts: Riley has a neck wound from Cholo's crossbow attack.
Slack removes her bandana and uses it to clean Riley's neck wound.
Riley makes eye contact with Slack and registers the emotional weight of her gesture.

memories.memory_text: I traced my finger along the monitor, mapping the route above the Throat—no bridges, no warzone. We come in high, avoid the fighting, and open a way out. That fence wasn’t just a barrier. It was a cage.
grounded_facts: Riley examines a map on a monitor showing the Golden Triangle and surrounding rivers.
Riley identifies 'The Throat' as the base of the triangle where a long fence runs between two rivers.
Riley proposes an infiltration route above the Throat to avoid urban combat and assist civilians trapped inside.

memories.memory_text: I sprinted up the tilting roadbed and jumped the widening gap just before the bridge dropped too far, landing hard and rolling as I hit the other side.
grounded_facts: Riley climbs the incline of the lowering drawbridge.
Riley leaps across the narrowing gap to the opposite side.
Riley lands, falls, rolls, and gets up to run after landing.

memories.memory_text: I ran out from the tree line and saw the Throat—people screaming, getting torn apart, the fence sparking as bodies hit it. The air was thick with smoke and the smell of burning flesh.
grounded_facts: Riley arrives at the Throat at night after running from a line of trees. The Throat is filled with trapped people being attacked by hundreds of zombies. Electrified fencing is active
causing sparks and electrocuting bodies. Screams and chaos are present in the environment. Sensory details include the sounds of screaming and the visual of sparking fences.

memories.memory_text: A dead thing lunged at me—three feet away—so I shot it in the head. Blood sprayed, lit up by the headlights of Dead Reckoning just before it crushed the body. Then I stepped into its path.
grounded_facts: Riley is confronted by a zombie at close range (three feet away). He responds by shooting it in the skull with a pistol. Blood from the shot is backlit by Dead Reckoning's headlights. The vehicle runs over the zombie's body. Riley deliberately stands in the vehicle's path after the kill.

memories.memory_text: I didn't flinch as the vehicle bore down on me—then everything went black. Next thing I know, I'm clawing my way up the windshield, hands scraping against glass, Harlie screaming my name.
grounded_facts: Riley did not move as the vehicle approached rapidly.
Riley disappeared from view when the vehicle reached him.
Riley reappeared seconds later, climbing across the windshield.
Harlie shouted 'RILEY!' in anguish after Riley disappeared.

memories.memory_text: I leapt off the gun the second before the charge blew—felt the heat of the explosion on my back as I hit the ground running, the first through the breach.
grounded_facts: Riley was riding the fourteen-incher before the explosion. Riley jumped off just before detonation. The fences were destroyed by an explosion. Riley entered the breach immediately after the explosion. The scene occurred at night with smoke and chaos following the blast. Riley moved forward before others
going against the fleeing crowd.

memories.memory_text: I was firing blind into the dark, dropping Walkers as they grabbed people—saved a woman with a baby, a couple, a priest—until the gun clicked empty and the horde started closing in.
grounded_facts: Walkers attacked civilians behind the crowd. Riley fired in multiple directions. Multiple civilians were saved: a woman with a baby
a young couple
and a priest. Riley's weapon ran out of ammunition. Walkers surrounded Riley after the ammo ran out. The environment was chaotic with smoke and surging movement.

memories.memory_text: I saw Brian's face covered in blood after they mowed down the dead things, and when Mulligan asked me to rejoin, I just shook my head and told him it was all his.
grounded_facts: Riley witnessed the revolutionaries, including Brian, killing walkers at The Throat.
Brian's face was speckled with the blood of the dead.
Mulligan offered Riley a place back in the revolution.
Riley declined by saying 'It's all yours, Mulligan.'
The decision followed Riley observing the violence and Brian's bloodied state.

memories.memory_text: I lowered the binoculars and said it out loud—'All they want is somewhere to go. Same as us.' It hit me then, clear and quiet, that we weren't so different.
grounded_facts: Riley lowers binoculars.
Riley speaks the line: 'All they want is somewhere to go. Same as us.'

memories.memory_text: I looked at Slack, then told Pretty Boy to take us North—no hesitation, just the order, and they followed.
grounded_facts: Riley issued a command to go North aboard Dead Reckoning.
The group, including Slack, Anchors, Scar, and Pillsbury, did not object.
Riley addressed Pretty Boy directly to steer the vehicle.

memories.memory_text: I reached out and touched the paint on Dead Black’s face, then looked at my fingers—now stained red.
grounded_facts: Slack touched Dead Black's face
Slack's fingers were stained red after touching Dead Black

memories.memory_text: They threw me in that cage like meat, and I knew—right then—I had to move, had to fight, had to stay alive even if they already called me Dead Red.
grounded_facts: Slack was placed in the iron-mesh cage while alive and visibly bruised
Slack was designated as 'Dead Red' and introduced as bait among the painted walkers

memories.memory_text: I told Riley the truth about why I was in the arena—it wasn't the little man, it was the one upstairs. I told him I was working with Mulligan, trying to push back against the system that keeps us all down, chewing on bones while they eat steak.
grounded_facts: Slack reveals that the 'big man' orchestrated his placement in the arena as punishment.
Slack states he was working with Mulligan and his people.
Slack uses the metaphor of 'eating steak' versus 'getting the bones' to describe systemic inequality.

memories.memory_text: I asked Riley to take me when he escapes—wherever he’s going. I told him I can shoot and be useful, that I had Army training before they decided I was better off as a hooker. I needed him to see I wasn’t just dead weight.
grounded_facts: Slack directly asks Riley to include him in his escape plan.
Slack emphasizes his combat skills and prior military training.
Slack references being denied Army service due to exploitation.

memories.memory_text: The cell door clanged open and a guard asked for Denbo—I didn't move, but my pulse jumped. Riley, Charlie, and I all looked at each other, silent, wondering what this meant now.
grounded_facts: A Fiddler's Green security guard opened the cell door and called out for Denbo.
Slack was present in the cell with Riley and Charlie when the guard arrived.
Slack exchanged glances with Riley and Charlie in response to the guard's question.

memories.memory_text: I said I'd take the gun, and when the soldier asked if I could handle it, I told him I could—better than him, and pointed out the safety was off. He had to click it on before handing it over.
grounded_facts: Slack requested the weapon from the soldier
Slack stated she could handle the weapon better than the soldier
Slack noticed and pointed out that the weapon's safety was off
The soldier verified the safety was off, then turned it on before giving her the gun

memories.memory_text: I tied my hair back, checked my clip, and said I'd feel like a dick if I didn't go—Riley was asking if we wanted in, and I knew I had to move, even if I didn't give a damn about the place.
grounded_facts: Slack tied her hair back with a bandana
Slack checked her gun clip
Slack stated she would feel like a 'dick' if she didn't join the mission
Slack expressed indifference toward the mission objective but committed anyway

memories.memory_text: I saw the stenches had torn through the fence, and beyond that—suburb, river, the Fiddler's Green tower glowing in the distance. That's when it hit me: they're not just wandering. They want the city.
grounded_facts: Slack observes the stenches have breached the depot fence
Slack sees the suburb, river, and Fiddler's Green high-rise beyond the breach
Slack concludes the stenches intend to take the city

memories.memory_text: Charlie wet the sight of his rifle with his thumb, whispering it catches the moonlight so he can aim. I didn't get why he'd do something like that—seemed strange, ritualistic—so I asked him, quiet, 'Why do you do that?'
grounded_facts: Slack observes Charlie wetting the rifle sight with his thumb
Slack hears Charlie whisper that it catches the light for aiming
Slack whispers the question 'Why do you do that?'

memories.memory_text: Charlie's shot barely missed me, but it took out the walker behind me—bullet buzzed my ear and blew a wisp of hair. I felt the heat of it, heard the crack, then saw the dead man drop.
grounded_facts: Charlie fired his .45 and killed a walker behind Slack
the bullet passed close to Slack's ear
Slack acknowledged the save with 'Thanks
Charlie. That was useful.'

memories.memory_text: I shot the bridal walker just as it went for Charlie—my bullet clipped his ear, but dropped the thing in time. He yelled it hurt, but I called it even.
grounded_facts: Slack shot a female walker in a tattered bridal gown approaching Charlie
her bullet hit the walker and clipped Charlie's ear
Slack said 'Even-Steven' immediately after the shot

memories.memory_text: I was driving, and Riley told me to pull over out of nowhere—then he pointed his gun at Pillsbury and Manolete, laid it out plain: he wasn't saving the Green for anyone, he was taking the car and going to Canada. When I realized he'd been planning this all along, I said, 'No. I like the way you think,' because that’s what you do when someone shows they’re not playing the sucker.
grounded_facts: Slack was driving the T-Bird when Riley instructed him to pull over.
Riley revealed his intention to keep Dead Reckoning and go to Canada, not return it as expected.
Riley held Pillsbury and Manolete at gunpoint to assert control over the mission's direction.
Slack responded with the line, 'No. I like the way you think,' indicating a shift in his perception of Riley and the mission.

memories.memory_text: I was driving the Impala down the country road at night when Riley suddenly said Dead Reckoning was heading to Ross Park. He said it's the powder magazine—where they keep the rockets for the cannons.
grounded_facts: Slack was driving the Impala.
Riley was monitoring a device referred to as a "Game Boy".
Riley stated that Dead Reckoning was going to Ross Park.
Riley identified Ross Park as the location of a powder magazine containing rockets for cannons.

memories.memory_text: I yanked the T-Bird into the driveway hard, tires crunching gravel, and killed the engine under Riley’s shout—just in time to see the city lights sprawl below, knowing they’d be coming up that road soon.
grounded_facts: Slack pulled the T-Bird into a driveway on Riley's command.
The T-Bird stopped in the shadow of a house.
The city was visible from the hillside location.
Riley indicated the enemy would be coming up the road.

memories.memory_text: I asked Riley if he was really going out there alone—told him he’d need our guns, but he said Dead Reckoning’s got steel skin an inch thick and you can’t shoot your way in.
grounded_facts: Slack questioned Riley's decision to approach Dead Reckoning alone.
Slack suggested using firearms, believing force was necessary.
Riley responded that gunfire would be ineffective due to the vehicle's armor.
The exchange occurred while waiting in the shadows of a driveway at night.

memories.memory_text: I saw the hatch open from the shadows—we were close enough to see it, but not close enough to stop it. That’s when I knew waiting was over.
grounded_facts: Slack was present with Pillsbury in the shadows near a hillside home driveway at night.
Slack observed the hatch opening as a visible event from his position.
The opening of the hatch marked a shift in the situation, ending a period of observation.

memories.memory_text: I aimed my K-90 at Cholo through the rustling branches, voice steady—'That man pulls the trigger, he's dead.'
grounded_facts: Slack aimed her K-90 at Cholo
She spoke a warning directed at Cholo regarding Riley being held with a crossbow
She moved forward with Pillsbury and froze upon seeing the scene
Her movement caused rustling in the branches

memories.memory_text: I remember the guns on the vehicle starting to turn toward the city with this loud whirring sound — I knew we didn’t have much time left.
grounded_facts: The vehicle's guns began rotating toward the city with loud whirring sounds.
Slack spoke to Riley, expressing urgency and telling him to make himself useful.
Slack was present on the hillside at night and observed the vehicle's movement.

memories.memory_text: I shot Manolete in the head while the Walker was tearing into him—no hesitation. He had the gun on Cholo, and the moment was gone if I waited.
grounded_facts: Slack shot Manolete in the head
Manolete was being attacked by the Walker when shot
Manolete was aiming at Cholo at Dead Reckoning's open hatch
Riley pushed Cholo out of the way of incoming fire

memories.memory_text: After the shot, I opened fire on the Walker with Pillsbury—the two of us laying down fire until it was destroyed. I could see the thing bucking and sparking, then going still.
grounded_facts: Slack and Pillsbury opened fire on the Walker together
The Walker was destroyed by their combined fire
The Walker was actively attacking Manolete before being shot

memories.memory_text: I saw the orange glow across the river and told Riley to look—fire was rising from the city, and I knew the stenches had gotten through.
grounded_facts: Slack noticed the flickering orange glow coming from across the river.
Slack directed Riley's attention to the glow by saying 'Riley. Look out there.'
The glow was fire rising from the city, visible between the houses on the hillside.
The group concluded the stenches had breached the river and reached the city.

memories.memory_text: I pulled off my bandana to wipe the blood from Riley’s neck. He looked at me like it meant something—I almost lost him.
grounded_facts: Slack removes her hair bandana and uses it to clean blood from Riley's neck wound.
Riley is injured under the chin from a crossbow strike by Cholo.
Slack says 'Almost lost you there,' indicating concern.

memories.memory_text: I said we could blast our way through the barricades. It was a direct option—clear the path fast.
grounded_facts: Slack suggests using force to breach the barricaded bridges into the city.
She voices a tactical alternative to bypassing the Golden Triangle’s barriers.
Her suggestion is met with Riley’s rejection due to risk of entering an active conflict.

memories.memory_text: I jumped out right after Riley, and when she fired, I knew I had to do more than just follow—I had to act. Saying 'Making myself useful' wasn't just words; it was me stepping into it.
grounded_facts: Slack exited the vehicle immediately after Riley.
A Walker emerged from the darkness.
Riley shot the Walker.
Slack spoke the line 'Making myself useful' in response to the situation.

memories.memory_text: I saw the rat—half its body gone, ass blown off—but still skittering forward. Charlie didn't hit the head, and that's when I knew: same as the people, only a headshot stops them.
grounded_facts: The rat was shot and missing its lower half but continued moving.
Charlie stated he didn't hit the head, implying that was the reason it kept moving.
Slack realized the same rule applied to undead animals as to undead humans.

memories.memory_text: They came at me from the road—skreeing, swarming—so I vaulted the railing and dropped to the riverbank. But they were already down there too, crawling everywhere.
grounded_facts: A pack of zombie rats approached Slack on the roadbed.
Slack physically jumped over a railing to escape to the riverbank.
Zombie rats were also present on the riverbank, indicating no safe ground.

memories.memory_text: I felt the rats snap at my hands as I cut the tether—jaws closing on air just past my fingers—but I kept sawing at the rope with the jackknife until it frayed and gave.
grounded_facts: Slack used a jackknife to cut the tether binding the dinghy to the shore.
Rats attacked Slack's hands during the attempt, forcing her to recoil but not stop cutting.

memories.memory_text: Once the current took the dinghy, I was crouched on the upturned hull, adrift with no paddles and no way to steer—just the river pulling me into the dark.
grounded_facts: The dinghy was set free by the current after the tether was cut.
Slack had no means of propulsion or steering once on the water.

memories.memory_text: I reached out with my hand and it hit something long and slimy—thought it was a snake, but it wasn't. It was a rope, thick with moss, and I grabbed it hard, starting to pull.
grounded_facts: Slack's hand hit a long, slimy object while paddling with her hands
She initially mistook the moss-covered rope for a snake
She grabbed the rope and began pulling hand over hand toward the opposite shore

memories.memory_text: I was pulling on the rope when a face suddenly bobbed up out of the water—eyes blinking—floating right past me. It was the Bridgekeeper's head. I screamed.
grounded_facts: A face, identified as the Bridgekeeper's severed head, emerged from the water near Slack
The head had blinking eyes and was floating on the current
Slack screamed upon seeing it

memories.memory_text: I grabbed Brian as he ran past me, panicked—couldn't let him get caught in the open with those things closing in.
grounded_facts: Slack physically intervened by grabbing Brian during a moment of chaos.
Brian was moving independently and appeared distressed before being intercepted by Slack.

memories.memory_text: I ran with Brian past the manhole, flames swirling from the oil pots, workers throwing them like comets at the Walkers—no time to stop, just move.
grounded_facts: Slack moved with Brian through a hazardous area marked by flaming oil pots and active combat.
The environment included maintenance workers using improvised incendiary devices against Walkers near a manhole.

memories.memory_text: I helped pull open the cell doors after the keys turned in the locks, and Mulligan and his men came out.
grounded_facts: Slack helped open the cell doors.
Keys were used to unlock the doors before they were opened.
Brian also helped open the doors.
Mulligan and the revolutionaries emerged from the cells.

memories.memory_text: I whispered Riley's name in the dark, then stepped out from behind one of the dead things—he turned, and we moved toward each other through the frozen walkers, meeting in silence before the guns tore it all apart.
grounded_facts: Slack whispered Riley's name off-screen before emerging from behind a motionless walker.
Slack and Riley cautiously approached each other through a group of immobile dead things.
They embraced while surrounded by the statuesque walkers, just before Mulligan and his revolutionaries opened fire.

memories.memory_text: I said we’d take them with us—Charlie and Riley. The others didn’t object, but it was my call to make.
grounded_facts: Slack makes the final decision to include Charlie and Riley in the group's movement north.
The decision occurs after Riley asks if anyone has objections and looks at Slack.
Anchored by Slack's line: 'No, we'll take you along with us.'
